footer.disclaimer: CareLoop flags monitoring risks for professional review. It does not diagnose or prescribe.

landing.heroSub: WhatsApp check-ins, vital signals, and explainable rules help nurses monitor elderly chronic-care patients between clinic visits.
landing.safetyLine: Monitoring support only. Not diagnosis. No treatment recommendation.

preview.name: Mrs. Chan, 78

problem.title: The gap between visits
problem.sub: The risk is not only inside the clinic. It is the long period where symptoms, adherence, and daily changes become invisible.
problem.step2Body: Days to weeks pass with little visibility.
problem.step4Body: Condition worsens, costs go up, stress rises.
problem.bottleneck1: Elderly patients may not open new apps or remember daily check-ins.
problem.bottleneck2: Families and care teams have limited visibility.
problem.bottleneck3: Delays can lead to avoidable deterioration and urgent care.

how.title: How it works
how.sub: From a WhatsApp message to a nurse decision — four steps, no app for the patient to install.
how.step1Body: A daily Cantonese or English check-in. No app to install, no login to forget.
how.step2Body: Symptoms, weight, blood pressure, and voice notes become structured data.
how.step3Body: Explainable rules (HF-001, not a black box) decide what needs attention. No AI diagnosis — ever.
how.step4Body: An exception-first dashboard with a full audit trail. One nurse covers a whole ward.

features.title: Everything a monitoring program needs
features.sub: Built for clinical accountability, not engagement metrics.
features.f1Body: Conservative, explainable rules with rule IDs. Every escalation says exactly why.
features.f2Body: Cantonese and English over WhatsApp — the channel elderly patients already use.
features.f3Body: Nurses review the patients who need it, not all of them.
features.f4Body: Family is looped in on escalations, with nurse-controlled wording.
features.f5Body: Weekly summaries, PDF reports, and FHIR-style export into existing workflows.
features.f6Body: Every access and action recorded. Built for clinical accountability.

trust.item2: Encrypted in transit and at rest
trust.honesty: Not yet SOC 2 certified — we're early and say so. Security documentation is shared with every evaluating hospital.

cta.sub: A 30-minute walkthrough with our team — your use case, your language mix, your escalation rules.

business.title: Business case
business.sub: CareLoop turns between-visit monitoring into an operational workflow. Here is who buys it, why, and how it stays safe at scale.

pricing.title: Pricing that follows the engagement
pricing.sub: CareLoop is deployed per hospital, not per seat. We scope every engagement together — here is how it usually runs.
pricing.pilot1: One ward or care program, with a defined patient cohort
pricing.pilot2: Three months, fixed pilot fee scoped to your cohort
pricing.pilot3: Full product: WhatsApp check-ins, risk rules, nurse dashboard
pricing.pilot4: Joint success criteria and weekly review with our team
pricing.standard1: Per-patient, per-month subscription after a successful pilot
pricing.standard2: Unlimited nurse and admin seats — we never charge per clinician
pricing.standard3: Bilingual check-ins, caregiver alerts, PDF + FHIR-style export
pricing.standard4: Email support and product updates included
pricing.enterprise1: Dedicated instance: your own database, application, and WhatsApp number
pricing.enterprise2: Data residency in your preferred region — nothing shared with other hospitals
pricing.enterprise3: Custom escalation rules, SSO/SMTP integration, SLA
pricing.enterprise4: Optional self-hosted deployment inside your network
pricing.whyBody: Cohort size, language mix, and integration depth change the economics. We'd rather scope honestly in 30 minutes than publish a number that's wrong for your ward.

security.title: Security & trust
security.sub: How CareLoop protects patient data, in plain language. Questions we haven't answered here: ask us directly.
security.isolationBody: Every production hospital runs its own dedicated CareLoop instance — its own database, its own application deployment, its own WhatsApp number. Your patients' data never shares infrastructure with another organization. Not "logically separated" — physically separate.
security.accessBody: Staff accounts are provisioned by your administrator — there is no public signup. Two roles (administrator and nurse), and every page and API call re-checks the session on the server.
security.encryptionBody: TLS 1.2+ in transit. AES-256 at rest in the managed Postgres database. Service credentials never reach the browser.
security.auditBody: Every access and action is recorded and attributed to the signed-in user — exportable for internal review.
security.deterministicBody: Escalation severity is decided by explainable, versioned rules — never by an AI model. AI is used only to structure language (transcription, wording), never to judge clinical risk.
security.residencyBody: Each dedicated instance is provisioned in your preferred cloud region — for Hong Kong deployments, typically Singapore. (Our public demo runs in the US; production instances are placed where you need them.)
security.backupsBody: Daily automated backups are standard; point-in-time recovery is available on enterprise instances.
security.certsBody: CareLoop is not yet SOC 2 or ISO 27001 certified. We are early, and we won't pretend otherwise. Our roadmap: SOC 2 Type I, then Type II. We will share our security questionnaire answers and architecture documentation with any evaluating hospital.
security.reportingBody: Report security issues privately to the team — never through public issue trackers, and never with real patient data in the report.
security.ctaSub: We'll walk your IT and data-protection teams through everything.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.s1p: CareLoop provides monitoring support for chronic-care programs: it collects daily check-ins and vital readings, applies deterministic escalation rules, and surfaces results to the care team. CareLoop does not diagnose, prescribe, or replace clinical judgment.
privacy.s2p: On behalf of the deploying healthcare organization, CareLoop processes: patient identification details entered by the care team (name, age, conditions, caregiver contact), WhatsApp messages and voice-note transcripts sent to the check-in number, vital readings (weight, blood pressure, heart rate, activity, sleep), and the resulting risk evaluations. An append-only audit trail records every access and action for accountability.
privacy.s3h: Where it lives and who sees it
privacy.s3p: Data is stored in the organization's dedicated database (Supabase Postgres) and is scoped to that organization. Access requires a staff account provisioned by the organization's administrator. Voice notes are transcribed and free-text messages are structured using AI services (speech-to-text and language models); these services process message content only and never decide clinical severity.
privacy.s4p: The deploying organization controls retention and deletion. Patients and caregivers can direct questions, correction requests, or deletion requests to their care provider, who administers the data. Hong Kong's Personal Data (Privacy) Ordinance (PDPO) applies to deployments in Hong Kong.
privacy.s5p: For privacy questions about a specific deployment, contact the deploying organization's administrator.

terms.title: Terms of use
terms.s1p: CareLoop is monitoring support software. It flags monitoring signals for professional review using deterministic, auditable rules. It does not diagnose, prescribe, or provide treatment recommendations, and it is not a substitute for clinical judgment or emergency services. In an emergency, call local emergency services immediately.
terms.s2p: The nurse dashboard and patient records are restricted to staff accounts provisioned by the deploying organization's administrator. Accounts are personal; credentials must not be shared. The organization is responsible for revoking access when staff leave.
terms.s3p: Daily check-ins depend on third-party messaging (WhatsApp via Twilio) and patient response; the care team remains responsible for following up on missed check-ins. CareLoop is provided as part of a service agreement with the deploying organization, which governs uptime, support, and liability.
terms.s4p: Use of patient data is governed by the deploying organization's agreements and the

onboard.title: Activate your CareLoop check-in
onboard.sub: Two quick steps in WhatsApp — no app to install, no account.
onboard.step1Title: Scan the QR and hit send
onboard.step1Body: WhatsApp opens with "{joinText}" already typed. Just send it — this connects you to CareLoop.
onboard.step2Body: Reply with how you feel today — type, or send a Cantonese voice note (e.g. "今日有啲氣促，對腳腫咗"). CareLoop confirms your enrolment, and your patient record appears on the nurse dashboard for review.
onboard.whyTwo: Why two messages? The first connects you to the WhatsApp sandbox; the second is the first one CareLoop actually receives, so it learns your number (a scan alone never reveals it). From then on, CareLoop sends you the daily check-in first — you just reply.
onboard.disclaimer: CareLoop is monitoring support — not a diagnosis or treatment tool.

weekly.title: Weekly clinician summary
weekly.generatedAiDesc: Wording polished by Claude; figures are deterministic.

weekly.completeness: Data completeness: {pct}% · AI is used only for wording; severity and figures are deterministic.

fhir.title: FHIR-style export

csv.sub: Columns: date, weight_kg, systolic_bp, diastolic_bp, heart_rate, steps, sleep_hours, medication_taken, shortness_of_breath, swelling. Rows are imported for this patient and re-evaluated by the rule engine.
csv.emptyError: Choose a CSV file or paste rows first

rules.HF-001: Rapid weight gain can indicate fluid retention in heart-failure monitoring.
rules.HF-002: Weight gain combined with shortness of breath and swelling requires review.
rules.MED-001: Repeated missed medication increases chronic-care risk.
rules.BP-001: Very high blood pressure should be reviewed urgently.
rules.ACT-001: Reduced activity may signal deterioration or frailty risk.
rules.SYM-001: Patient reported symptoms that warrant nurse review, regardless of condition.

recommendedAction.escalate: Nurse review recommended today and notify family. If symptoms are severe, contact emergency services.
recommendedAction.review_today: Nurse review recommended today. Consider booking a clinic follow-up.
recommendedAction.watch: Continue monitoring. Nurse review if the trend continues.
recommendedAction.stable: No action needed. Continue routine monitoring.

metadata.rootDescription: CareLoop turns daily Cantonese check-ins, vital signals, and deterministic escalation rules into a nurse-review workflow for elderly Hong Kong patients between clinic visits. Monitoring support — not diagnosis.
metadata.ogDescription: Daily WhatsApp check-ins, deterministic risk rules, and an exception-first nurse dashboard for chronic care between clinic visits.
metadata.landingDescription: WhatsApp check-ins, vital signals, and explainable deterministic rules help nurses monitor elderly chronic-care patients between clinic visits. Monitoring support — not diagnosis.
metadata.businessDescription: Who pays for CareLoop and why: elderly-care operators, home-care agencies, clinics and insurers — KPIs, risks at scale, and mitigations.
metadata.pricingDescription: How CareLoop engagements work: pilot program, per-patient subscription, and dedicated enterprise instances. No public price list — every hospital's scope differs.
metadata.securityDescription: How CareLoop protects patient data: dedicated instances per hospital, encryption, audit trails, and deterministic clinical rules.
metadata.contactDescription: Tell us about your ward or program — pilots, pricing, and security reviews. A real person replies within one business day.
metadata.privacyDescription: How CareLoop handles patient monitoring data.
metadata.termsDescription: Terms of use for the CareLoop monitoring platform.
